evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot clearly shows three classes (up/down/ns) with threshold dashed lines and top-5 gene annotations; scientific claim (differential expression) is immediately readable.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; PDF uses Type3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans fonts, making text non-selectable/editable; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Grey/red/blue palette is colorblind-safe (IBM/Wong-friendly) and directionally appropriate (blue=down, red=up, grey=ns); minor issue: red/green avoided correctly.
evidence.layout_density: 7x5.5 inch figure uses space well; tight_layout applied; no overlapping labels (path_effects white stroke); y-range data-driven; minimal wasted space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and counts; legend placed upper-right (acceptable); gene labels are positional indices, not real names—caption acknowledges this; no major Reviewer 2 red flags.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type3 (bitmap) fonts (Subtype /Type3 in PDF stream) because fonttype=42 was not set; scatter points are rasterized=True; text is not selectable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial (183 KB PDF, 100 KB PNG, 139 KB SVG, 563 B caption, 2.3 KB script).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption specifies exact counts (up n=235, down n=230, ns n=2535), threshold values, and explains the annotation strategy; complete and informative.

overall_strengths: Clear, well-structured volcano plot with correct classification logic, threshold lines, and top-5 annotation with white-stroke path effects preventing overlap.
Excellent caption quality with specific numerical claims (gene counts, threshold values) and honest disclosure about gene label provenance.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 (or rcParams equivalent) set, so the PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type3 bitmap fonts—text is non-editable and the figure fails journal vector-text requirements.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout; no rcParams customization for a publication-quality typeface (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a serif option).
